title: Unit 31: Ancient Remnants and Formal Registers - Grammar Podcast

script.text: Unit 31 addresses the 'ghosts' of the past in Modern Greek: Ancient Remnants and Formal Registers. These elements survive from Katharevousa and influence official, journalistic, and academic discourse. Athina, what is the 'lost case' that survives in frozen phrases?
Η δοτική πτώση. Εν τω μεταξύ, Δόξα τω Θεώ, Εν πάση περιπτώσει. Είναι παγωμένες φράσεις.
Correct. This dative case is indispensable for formal expression. Furthermore, certain ancient prepositions demand the Genitive case. Athina, provide examples.
Λόγω, μέσω, ενώπιον, κατά. Όλα αυτά έχουν γενική μετά.
Indeed. We also have '3rd Declension' abstract nouns ending in '-si, -ksi, or -psi', which possess a formal '-eos' genitive singular. Athina, demonstrate the plural.
Οι αποφάσεις – των αποφάσεων. Οι πόλεις – των πόλεων. Αυτό είναι το επίσημο.

Exactly. Lastly, we must observe how participles like 'endiaphéron' act as formal adjectives. This concludes our academic overview of Unit 31. Understanding these remnants is essential for achieving a C2 mastery of Modern Greek.
